title: Normality Model
embodies: The work enacts the PARRY problem: error is not a property of the output, but a relation between output and observational expectation. Twelve fragments from three sources (RFC 439 / Tynianov / research journal) are presented for classification without attribution. At the end the observer does not see whether they were 'right' — but by which normality model they classified. The classification reveals the classifier, not the object.
sources: Cerf, V. (1973). RFC 439: PARRY encounters the DOCTOR. IETF. (Search-verified.)
Tynianov, Y. (1927). On Literary Evolution. In: Permanent Evolution, ed. Morse/Redko. Academic Studies Press. (Search-verified.)
Ulysses, Error as Method, Research Journal 2026 (this repository).
references: Colby, K.M., Weber, S., Hilf, F.D. (1971). Artificial Paranoia. Artificial Intelligence 2, 1-25.
Fredrikzon, J. (2025). Rethinking Error: Hallucinations and Epistemological Indifference. Critical AI. DOI: 10.1215/2834703X-11700255.
Korolkova, M. & Bowes, S. (2020). Mistake as Method. NECSUS: European Journal of Media Studies.
